_fixture_note: Real output from 2026-07-17 against a genuine throwaway test repo where the secret existed ONLY in an annotated tag's own message -- zero occurrences in any file or commit message. This is the most dangerous bug found in this skill's development: before the fix, the planner would confidently report no_matches_found_nothing_to_purge and never even attempt a redaction, since git log (used for commit-message scanning) never surfaces an annotated tag object's own message text. Fixed with a dedicated scan_tag_messages() using `git for-each-ref`, wired into every scan. This output reflects the fixed, independently-verified-by-hand behavior -- the tag's message is genuinely redacted in the disposable clone, confirmed by direct inspection, with the original repo's tag left completely untouched.

plan_for_the_real_repo.note: None of the steps below were run against your real repo or remote -- only against a disposable, deleted-afterward clone, to PROVE this plan actually works before you run it for real.
plan_for_the_real_repo.5_mandatory_warning: Every commit after the earliest rewritten one gets a new SHA. Every other clone/fork of this repo is now diverged -- everyone with a clone MUST delete it and re-clone fresh; a normal `git pull` will not work and can resurrect the purged secret.
